titleStmt.title: SII 25.4: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 4. (A.R. No. 4 of 1908.) GARIKEPĀḌU-AGRAHĀRA, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: Circular cup of white marble in a private house.

This Brāhmī inscription, partly embossed and partly engraved may be assigned to the first century B.C., on grounds of palaeography. It seems to record that the work (koḍaṁbaṁ) of a rail (sūchilā) was of a merchant (āpaṇika) named Datta (Data). It mentions that it was his gift.
The first two letters do not form part of the inscription. They were by mistake chiselled in the wrong way. The sentence too did not convey the correct idea of the gift it runs.
Dabhaka Apanikasa dānaṁ-The gift of Dabha, the merchant (āpaṇika) What follows is the correct record and runs.
Dabhasa Āpanikasa sūchikōḍabaṁ-The last word is not clear. Sūchi is the railing. The translation must be the cup which fits into the top of (the post in) the railing (a gift) of Dabha, the merchant.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: It is significant that the first letter is engraved while the succeeding letters are in embossed charters, though the anusvāra in naṁ is engraved.datasa Āpaṇikasa dānaṁ . The rest of the record is engraved.datasa Āpaṇikasa The form of sU is interesting and is met with in the Bhārhut inscription.sūcilā koḍabaṁ Read koḍaṁbaṁ.
Digital edition of SII 25.4 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
